title: Pithivier
subtitle: Domed almond cream pastry with a scored spiral
excerpt: A classic French pastry: two discs of puff pastry encasing a rich almond cream filling, with a scored spiral pattern on top. The scoring is the signature. Made from ready-made puff pastry.
sourceNotes: The pithivier takes its name from the town of Pithiviers in the Loiret, documented in French pâtisserie from the nineteenth century. The recipe (laminated pastry encasing almond cream) is fixed: there is no regional variation in the fundamental formula. This version uses ready-made puff pastry in place of the traditional hand-laminated feuilletage.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best eaten within 2 hours of baking. The pastry softens on standing.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf — puff pastry needs high heat

glossaryTerms.definition: Drawing a decorative pattern on the surface of puff pastry with the tip of a sharp knife, cutting only 2–3 mm deep into the top layer without going all the way through. The score marks open as the pastry puffs and bakes, creating a decorative pattern. Cutting too deep breaks the lamination and prevents even rising.

content.text: A pithivier is assembled from two discs of pastry sandwiching a mound of almond cream. The top disc is
in a curved spiral before baking — the pattern is the visual signature of the pastry. The knife must be cold and very sharp, and the scoring done after the egg wash, not before.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale. Beat in the {{free-range-eggs}} (for the filling) and {{vanilla-extract}}. Fold in the {{ground-almonds}}, {{plain-flour}}, and {{almond-extract}}. Refrigerate for 20 minutes.

Unroll the first sheet of {{puff-pastry}} and cut a 24 cm disc using a plate as a guide. Place on a lined baking tray. Unroll the second sheet and cut a 26 cm disc — slightly larger for the domed top.

Mound the cold almond cream in the centre of the base disc, leaving a 4 cm border. Brush the border with the beaten {{free-range-eggs}} (for egg wash). Lay the larger disc over the top and press the edges together firmly. Trim to neaten if needed. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Brush the top of the pithivier with egg wash. Using the tip of a very sharp knife, score curved lines from the centre outward in a spiral — 8 arcs, each curving from the centre to the edge, cutting 2–3 mm deep. Do not cut through. Bake for 28–32 minutes until deep golden and fully puffed. Serve warm.
